title: Tournament provider and tournament repositories
description: Create repository interfaces and SQLite implementations for tournament_providers and tournaments, following the existing repository pattern (e.g. division.go). Include standard CRUD plus domain-specific queries: visible tournaments ordered by display_order, tournaments by provider, and a FindByCourthiveTournamentID for sync upsert logic.
acceptance_criteria: TournamentProviderRepository interface with FindAll, FindByID, FindByAbbr, Create, Update, Delete
TournamentRepository interface with FindAll, FindByID, FindByProviderID, FindVisible, FindByCourthiveTournamentID, Create, Update, Delete
FindVisible returns tournaments where is_visible=TRUE ordered by display_order, start_date
Repositories registered in the application wiring (main.go or wherever repositories are initialised)

approach: 2. TournamentProviderRepository: standard CRUD + FindByAbbr for sync
3. TournamentRepository: standard CRUD + FindVisible (public index), FindByProviderID (admin list), FindByCourthiveTournamentID (upsert during sync)
4. Wire repositories into the app startup

testing_requirements: CRUD operations work correctly for both repositories
FindVisible only returns is_visible=TRUE tournaments in correct order
FindByAbbr returns nil/error for non-existent provider abbreviation
FindByCourthiveTournamentID returns nil/error for non-existent ID

context.user_story: As a developer, I need data access layers for tournaments so the service and handler layers can read/write tournament data
